description: Engine-evaluated screening threshold gate. The criteria carry the institution-configured clearThreshold (the documented, risk-based score cutoff below which a screen auto-clears); the performer submits the observedScore the screening engine returned as completion evidence. The rules engine settles FULFILLED when observedScore <= clearThreshold (auto-clear, no human needed) and FAILED otherwise (above the clear band, so a human dispositions the hit via keystone-alert-disposition-v1). The engine decides review-required from the score, which replaces an agent-asserted needsReview/isHit boolean an agent could set false to launder a hit past human review. clearThreshold is operator policy, not an agent guess: the institution configures the cutoff and keeps calibration evidence (an un-tuned threshold is a regulatory finding). On FAILED, open a keystone-alert-disposition-v1 and set screeningGateRef to this record id to route the hit to a Level-1 analyst. Create this gate with tolerance:0 (the recommended default): a nonzero auto-gate tolerance widens the band (observedScore <= clearThreshold + tolerance) and would silently auto-clear a hit past human review, re-introducing the dodge this engine gate exists to prevent. The contract type cannot itself forbid tolerance (it is a record-creation parameter, not a schema field), so the orchestrator supplies tolerance:0.

screenRef.description: recordId of the keystone-sanctions-screen-v1 whose score is being banded.

clearThreshold.description: Institution-configured auto-clear cutoff (0.0-1.0). Scores at or below clear automatically; scores above require human disposition.

observedScore.description: The top match score the screening engine returned for this case (keystone-sanctions-screen-v1.topScore).
